text:
Once upon an autumn afternoon. There was a wise old owl. He lived in an ancient oak tree. He learned many things.

One day he flew north to visit an old wise turtle. The turtle knew many things. He told stories about life.

With feathers dark as midnight the owl listened. Sometimes he thought they felt just as strange.

Soon they flew south. The owl thought of adventures. Tales of foxes.

The turtle liked those tales. You could imagine he prowled those forests.

Later that evening the owl liked orange fruits.

Somewhere deep. Maybe where owls think.

That's where he grew old.


That's okay. With kind thoughts he liked them still.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
